data.body:
G4 container child-consistency re-audit (parent f89d × THIS child 2359), cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57) and re-verified against the repo. CORROBORATES the two prior G4 findings on this ticket; both remain high-confidence and repo-grounded.

Repo re-verification:
- applier.py L816-820: the batch-path `RECON: batch_outcome` line emits only `action=…/key=…/error=…` (no per-sub-op counts); code comment calls it "the dominant outbound CREATE + UPDATE channel today" — this is the production path 2359 must instrument.
- apply_outbound.py (typed leaf): `links_applied`/`comments_applied` are ints (L325,278) but `labels_applied` is `list[str]` (L245,389); the existing no-op skip-guard (L372-378) already lives here, on the path 33d0 may delete OR make production ("Either delete the dead typed leaf … or make it the single production path and route the batch dispatch through it").
- apply_inbound.py L469/497: inbound `links_applied` is an int — the "parity" model 2359 cites is int-valued, reinforcing the labels list-vs-count mismatch (Finding 2).

Neither 2359 nor 33d0 declares a depends_on; they partition WORK (each lists the other as Out) but not the SHARED FILE STATE (batch_dispatch.py / applier.py) or the WHICH-PATH-SURVIVES assumption. Findings re-emitted unchanged: (1) conflicting-assumption + ordering + implicit-shared-state between 2359 and 33d0; (2) conflicting-assumption labels count-vs-list. Suggested resolution unchanged: sequence 33d0 before 2359 (add explicit depends_on) and/or fix the canonical surviving production path in both tickets and fold the counter/canary into 33d0's single survivor; specify label normalization (len()/derived count) so the invariant is well-defined without breaking the existing list-valued payload key.
